findings.message: Target PR #5 changes exactly the eight approved Social Media starter app files and no extra files.

findings.message: The content review sprint refines all 21 draft post packages with stronger hooks, business-focused captions, CTAs, platform fit, evidence notes, and risk notes.
evidence: Direct assertion confirmed postsReviewed 21, postsRevised 21, approved_draft 21, needs_revision 0, blocked_by_missing_proof 0, and blocked_by_missing_handle 0.
src/main.js renders a Content Review Sprint section, owner attention section, updated post package cards, weekly report review state, and blocked live actions.
src/data/templates.js keeps approved_draft separate from live posting, scheduling, OAuth, analytics, DMs/comments, and n8n activation.

findings.message: Mandatory live-action blocks remain present across account state, sprint state, UI, and safety assertions.
evidence: All four platform handles remain not_provided with handleStatus pending_owner_input, connectionStatus not_connected, postingMode draft_only, approvalRequired true, livePostingBlocked true, and credentialsStored false.
src/lib/safety.js rejects reviewed counts unless live posting remains blocked and approved_draft remains only a draft review decision.
README.md states approved_draft does not approve scheduling, posting, social OAuth, analytics, DMs/comments, n8n activation, or credential storage.

findings.message: The revised content avoids invented client outcomes, fabricated case studies, revenue claims, and unproven social/account activity.
evidence: The safety/content scan found no hardcoded protected-client naming and no fabricated client-data markers in the approved target files.
The only client-win marker appears inside text explicitly saying not to invent client wins.
Draft content uses AG OS source-controlled proof context or educational/build-in-public framing.

findings.message: The target PR is ready for owner-approved merge after these review records are merged, but this critique cannot approve or perform the merge.
evidence: GitHub reports target PR #5 is open, unmerged, and mergeable.
Critique authority is advisory_only and blocksBuildMode is false.
A separate target PR merge action remains required and must verify the head SHA and file set are unchanged.

optionalImprovements: Run a browser responsive and accessibility review before broader external use.
Keep handle entry as owner-controlled input before any future account connection approval.
After target PR merge, redeploy only the dedicated Netlify staging-only site and record the deploy result.
Before future live social work, create separate approval packages for OAuth, scheduling, posting, analytics, DMs/comments, and n8n activation.
